tokens: Our best guess is a heading of 274 for the first leg; 0.7 kilometers should take you past Buster there and just north of Spook.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Here I come, (getting on) the old Rover for the first time.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, am I sitting up in the air!
(Pause) Did you get your seat belt done?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Got it hooked to something, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got 118 ampere-hours, batteries are up to 90 and 90 (degrees F).
The forward and rear motor temps are off-scale low; amps are zero; and the volts are 68.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Do you now have the Nav and range.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: 033 is the bearing; the range is one-tenth.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hasn't changed any, has it (since John parked at the ALSEP site)?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Looking off to the northwest there (means southwest), you can see South Ray Crater with just tremendous amount of blocks on it with some black streaks, and here we go.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it's going to be a piece of cake taking pictures from here, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There's about 10-meter (diameter crater) off to your left there, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Four clicks an hour Tony and...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll end up in that big crater.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay at 043 and 0.2, just beyond the ALSEP, there are two twin craters.
The biggest one's to the north.
It's got blocks in it, up to 50 centimeters, and it's about 5 meters deep.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Say again what your best guess is to this thing?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now one thing I can't do is see the maps.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm not sure that heading is good.
That was based on an earlier estimate of where ALSEP was, and your bearing now may make that wrong.
It's pretty much if you go west you are going to hit Spook.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's what we're doing, going west.
I'm not sure that's not it right there, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That couldn't be it, could it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Man, this is the only way to go, riding this Rover.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You can hear the motors going, Tony, (through the suit).
Okay, we're still in this boulder field, on a heading of 300 now; just navigating around a couple of craters.
All of them look the same: these breccia clasts with a dark matrix with white clasts.
Biggest one I see is about, oh, in the 12 o'clock position of the Rover, and we're 065/0.2 and it's about a meter across.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, we seem to be riding across a ridge top that trends east-west.
Off to the left, it drops off drastically, about maybe a 5-to-10-degree slope into a valley which is probably Hidden Valley.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And South Ray Crater is in our 10 o'clock position; and we're 072 at 0.3 now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Do you have a speed amp?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Could we have the speed and the amps?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We got to go around this, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, you're 5 kilometers an hour, and the amps are oscillating about...between 10 and 20.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm really cinched into this moose.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, but I don't know with these holes if we ought to do that or not.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we're right on the timeline.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We see some small fresh craters, meter size, that show some very fresh...at least...Perhaps it's indurated regolith.
That's what it looks like, because the little hard clods are the same inside the craters as on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: At our 11 o'clock position, we're at 089 for 0.4.
We have two very bright, small craters that are 2 or 3 meters across, and we see some whitish material down below in the walls of the crater there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Those rocks that you collected (at the ALSEP), were they...Were they all breccias, or could you tell (what they were)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I think they were breccias, but they were really dust covered, so I couldn't tell you, really.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And have you seen any rocks that you're certain aren't breccias?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I haven't seen any that I'm convinced is not a breccia.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're going generally west now, and at our 1 o'clock position on a heading of 270 at a bearing...We're (at a bearing to the LM of) 091 at 0.5 (km range); we're in another distinct ray field - ray pat(tern) - 'Ray', let's say, 'boulder field'.
We sort of passed out of one, and we're in another one.
And we're getting the...Go ahead, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Think that to the south of us is Spook?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You should be just about to Spook.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) At 0.6 we should be at Spook, huh?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This ray field has the same pebbles (about 0.4 cm to 6.4 cm) and cobbles (about 6.4 cm to 25.6 cm) and some good secondaries here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That summary you got there, Charlie, (on the
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: John's just doing great driving this moose.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...is the point by Buster; you should be coming up to the edge of Spook.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Turn left, John, and let's go look down over there.
Boy, Tony, there is some excellent little secondaries with the indurated regolith in 'em and on the rim.
The biggest one is a couple of meters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know that might have been Spook right back there.
That was a pretty big crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A layered (rock), really dust covered, like a regolith (breccia)...I mean a...Turn left, John.
There's a crater over there, a big one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And Buster's right over here, with some blocks around it, to my right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, here is Spook; and it's 089 at 0.7.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're not...We're looking...We're almost completely past it; we're not right even with it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I thought it was right over here, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Is the rim of Spook rocky?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Answering Tony) Negative, it's real subdued, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, do you see any ledges or anything in the inside of Spook?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I think we're coming up on the (southeast) rim of
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: With some frags that we'll be able to get off.
The biggest boulder's a meter...(There are) cobbles.
It's real good for raking here.
That's it, Buster, there it is.
Okay, in Buster, Tony, I can see some HUGE boulders in the bottom of that thing!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Tony, we've got some 5-meter boulders in the bottom of it.
The biggest, 5 meters, and the whole bottom is covered; we're going downslope now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, there should be a scarp around there someplace.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's going to be great (sampling), Tony.
We'll be at...(Responding to Tony) Okay, we see it.
Over to the (at) our 2 o'clock position, and it looks like the rim of a crater, but I think it's a scarp.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What's the heading from here to...(Pause) Oh, man.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we want to head...Just keep going west.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Driving down-Sun in zero phase is murder.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To John) And you're making great time, though.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and in this area, the regolith is real smooth.
The cobble population is distinctly smaller and we're...Eek!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There it is, there's the Buster; I mean, there's Flag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, 088 at 1.0 is...(Stops to listen to Tony) Okay, we stop, John, about 40 meters from Plum.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, now, I don't see Plum.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That ain't even on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, it is; it's...Yeah, it is.
It's right...We're...The rim is right here.
We're on top of the rim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's going to be terrible walking on this thing.
Why don't we go turn around and go back up on the rim where it's level?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is a steep slope here.
It didn't seem like there was that much distance between (Buster and Flag) (Pause) 300 meters is Flag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Could we have another range and bearing, please?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, that's a big crater anyway.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, it looks to me like we're due north of South Ray Crater right now.
I can look down there, and I feel like I'm bisecting it.
No, we're not due north of it, not according to shadow.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, let's get off and start working.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, it was 0.6 between Buster and...That's got to be Buster.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It'll do...Let's not waste any more time thinking about it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're going to call this Flag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It has all the characteristics (of Flag).
We must have landed...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: When you're brushing off the LCRU there, would you get the TV lens, please?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I tell you, when you go to get on this thing (meaning the Rover), you better turn your cooling down or you'll freeze.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I should have gone to
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What we've got here is a...We think we're...Plum Crater is sitting right on the outer rim of Flag.
And it's what appears to me to be 200 meter...(Pause) Pretty big crater we call Flag, and this is an identical spot for...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Another big one right back here (garbled)...See that big one back up there that we called...This might be Halfway, this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, why don't we get back (on the Rover) and try some more, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I know what it is; every time I put the...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Belt on (and) tighten down the belt, it pulls my...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me get downslope, and you get upslope.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, it all looks the same, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Based on your knowledge of our position, give us where you think Flag is.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It looks like Plum Crater would be almost due west of you about 200 meters.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Responding to Tony) That's what we're doing.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think that's what we're doing.
We got a little depth-size problem here and I think we'll figure it out here in a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And this crater here is probably South...Correction: Halfway, with a smaller one on the side.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I tell you that is a bigger crater than that, though.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you go to the right, John?
There's a pretty fresh batch, dead ahead.
That's about a tenth of a kilometer across there, though; that's a big crater right there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I don't think it's a 300 meterer (sic).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I feel real faith in this thing (meaning the Rover); open her up a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can't see where I'm going, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, it takes those little bumps...Oh, it won't take that one; but it'll take those little ones just great.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're coming into another block-ray field up ahead of us about 50 meters or so, with angular blocks.
The area we have here now is almost cobble free, except perhaps less than 1 percent of the surface.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And now we're...There's another ray we're coming in out of South Ray, which (is) definitely out of South Ray, you can see it trending right on in to South Ray.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, that's that big South-Ray-Crater ray, down here, I think.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You can see partially into Stubby.
The Cinco Craters are very visible up there on the side (of Stone Mountain).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Got to go back right, John, to get (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...when you're in a ray or just the cobbles (about 64 mm to 256 mm) and boulders (bigger than about 256 mm)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: There is no mistake in your mind when you're in a ray because of the blocks.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And the surface is a little bit lighter - the regolith.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'd like to sort of tack.
I can see a lot better.
Just a little north or a little south.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Do you see both the white (brecciated anorthosite) and the black and white (breccia), here?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's mostly gray, Tony, with a...There's a big crater over there, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're coming into the south of Plum.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, this is probably Plum right over here...No, I guess not.
Plum is 40 meters; that's not nearly 40 meters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, I think we finally found Spook, here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, did you notice that scarp that's mapped, that you should have driven across?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's all hilly and scarpy here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, do you notice a trend to the scarps?
(On the pre-mission maps) they all seem to be (trending to the) northeast.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, that's probably pretty close, northeast.
(Pause) Okay, that's got to be it, John.
Unless this is it right here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We are on the rim of it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we got to park on the other side about 40 meters up.
(Pause) Well, Tony, we finally found it at 087 and 1.4.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You guys had us mapped just right then.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, you can't believe this territory.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're gonna be a little close here (to Plum), John, but that's okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Tony, we're parking right on the rim of Plum.
transcript.speaker: Duke
